NKJV.1: A Song of Ascents. Out of the depths I have cried to You, O LORD;
NKJV.2: Lord, hear my voice! Let Your ears be attentive To the voice of my supplications.
NKJV.3: If You, LORD, should mark iniquities, O Lord, who could stand?
NKJV.4: But there is forgiveness with You, That You may be feared.
NKJV.5: I wait for the LORD, my soul waits, And in His word I do hope.
NKJV.6: My soul waits for the Lord More than those who watch for the morning— Yes, more than those who watch for the morning.
NKJV.7: O Israel, hope in the LORD; For with the LORD there is mercy, And with Him is abundant redemption.
NKJV.8: And He shall redeem Israel From all his iniquities.

ESV.1: Out of the depths I cry to you, O LORD!
ESV.2: O Lord, hear my voice! Let your ears be attentive to the voice of my pleas for mercy!
ESV.3: If you, O LORD, should mark iniquities, O Lord, who could stand?
ESV.4: But with you there is forgiveness, that you may be feared.
ESV.5: I wait for the LORD, my soul waits, and in his word I hope;
ESV.6: my soul waits for the Lord more than watchmen for the morning, more than watchmen for the morning.
ESV.7: O Israel, hope in the LORD! For with the LORD there is steadfast love, and with him is plentiful redemption.
ESV.8: And he will redeem Israel from all his iniquities.

NASB.1: A Song of Ascents. Out of the depths I have cried to You, O LORD.
NASB.2: Lord, hear my voice! Let Your ears be attentive To the voice of my supplications.
NASB.3: If You, LORD, should mark iniquities, O Lord, who could stand?
NASB.4: But there is forgiveness with You, That You may be feared.
NASB.5: I wait for the LORD, my soul does wait, And in His word do I hope.
NASB.6: My soul [waits] for the Lord More than the watchmen for the morning; [Indeed, more than] the watchmen for the morning.
NASB.7: O Israel, hope in the LORD; For with the LORD there is lovingkindness, And with Him is abundant redemption.
NASB.8: And He will redeem Israel From all his iniquities.

NLT.1:
From the depths of despair, O LORD,
I call for your help.
NLT.2:
Hear my cry, O Lord.
Pay attention to my prayer.
NLT.3:
LORD, if you kept a record of our sins,
who, O Lord, could ever survive?
NLT.4:
But you offer forgiveness,
that we might learn to fear you.
NLT.5:
I am counting on the LORD;
yes, I am counting on him.
I have put my hope in his word.
NLT.6:
I long for the Lord
more than sentries long for the dawn,
yes, more than sentries long for the dawn.
NLT.7:
O Israel, hope in the LORD;
for with the LORD there is unfailing love.
His redemption overflows.
NLT.8:
He himself will redeem Israel
from every kind of sin.

WEB.1: A Song of Ascents. Out of the depths I have cried to you, Yahweh.
WEB.2: Lord, hear my voice. Let your ears be attentive to the voice of my petitions.
WEB.3: If you, Yah, kept a record of sins, Lord, who could stand?
WEB.4: But there is forgiveness with you, therefore you are feared.
WEB.5: I wait for Yahweh. My soul waits. I hope in his word.
WEB.6: My soul longs for the Lord more than watchmen long for the morning; more than watchmen for the morning.
WEB.7: Israel, hope in Yahweh, for with Yahweh there is loving kindness. With him is abundant redemption.
WEB.8: He will redeem Israel from all their sins.

CSB17.1: A song of ascents. [1] Out of the depths I call to you, LORD! ⓛ
CSB17.2:
Lord, listen to my voice;

Israel, put your hope in the LORD. ⓓ
For there is faithful love with the LORD,
and with him is redemption in abundance. ⓔ
CSB17.8:
And he will redeem Israel

AMP.1: A Song of Ascents. Out of the depths [of distress] I have cried to You, O LORD.
AMP.2:
Lord, hear my voice!
Let Your ears be attentive
To the voice of my supplications.
AMP.3:
If You, LORD, should keep an account of our sins and treat us accordingly,
O Lord, who could stand [before you in judgment and claim innocence]?
AMP.4:
But there is forgiveness with You,
That You may be feared and worshiped [with submissive wonder].
AMP.5:
I wait [patiently] for the LORD, my soul [expectantly] waits,
And in His word do I hope.
AMP.6:
My soul waits for the Lord
More than the watchmen for the morning;
More than the watchmen for the morning.
AMP.7:
O Israel, hope in the LORD;
For with the LORD there is lovingkindness,
And with Him is abundant redemption.
AMP.8:
And He will redeem Israel
From all his sins.

MSG.1:
A pilgrim song
Help, God—the bottom has fallen out of my life!
Master, hear my cry for help!
MSG.2:
Listen hard! Open your ears!
Listen to my cries for mercy.
MSG.3:
If you, God, kept records on wrongdoings,
who would stand a chance?
MSG.4:
As it turns out, forgiveness is your habit,
and that's why you're worshiped.
MSG.5:
I pray to God—my life a prayer—
and wait for what he'll say and do.
MSG.6:
My life's on the line before God, my Lord,
waiting and watching till morning,
waiting and watching till morning.
MSG.7:
O Israel, wait and watch for God—
with God's arrival comes love,
with God's arrival comes generous redemption.
MSG.8:
No doubt about it—he'll redeem Israel,
buy back Israel from captivity to sin.

NIV.1:
Psalm 130
A song of ascents.
Out of the depths I cry to you, O Lord;
NIV.2:
O Lord, hear my voice.
Let your ears be attentive
to my cry for mercy.
NIV.3:
If you, O Lord, kept a record of sins,
O Lord, who could stand?
NIV.4:
But with you there is forgiveness;
therefore you are feared.
NIV.5:
I wait for the Lord, my soul waits,
and in his word I put my hope.
NIV.6:
My soul waits for the Lord
more than watchmen wait for the morning,
more than watchmen wait for the morning.
NIV.7:
O Israel, put your hope in the Lord,
for with the Lord is unfailing love
and with him is full redemption.
NIV.8:
He himself will redeem Israel
from all their sins.

NRSVCE.1: Out of the depths I cry to you, O LORD.
NRSVCE.2:
Lord, hear my voice!
Let your ears be attentive
to the voice of my supplications!
NRSVCE.3:
If you, O LORD, should mark iniquities,
Lord, who could stand?
NRSVCE.4:
But there is forgiveness with you,
so that you may be revered.
NRSVCE.5:
I wait for the LORD, my soul waits,
and in his word I hope;
NRSVCE.6:
my soul waits for the Lord
more than those who watch for the morning,
more than those who watch for the morning.
NRSVCE.7:
O Israel, hope in the LORD!
For with the LORD there is steadfast love,
and with him is great power to redeem.
NRSVCE.8:
It is he who will redeem Israel
from all its iniquities.

NET.1: From the deep water I cry out to you, O LORD.
NET.2: O Lord, listen to me! Pay attention to my plea for mercy!
NET.3: If you, O LORD, were to keep track of sins, O Lord, who could stand before you?
NET.4: But you are willing to forgive, so that you might be honored.
NET.5: I rely on the LORD, I rely on him with my whole being; I wait for his assuring word.
NET.6: I yearn for the Lord, more than watchmen do for the morning, yes, more than watchmen do for the morning.
NET.7: O Israel, hope in the LORD, for the LORD exhibits loyal love, and is more than willing to deliver.
NET.8: He will deliver Israel from all the consequences of their sins.
